experience.label: AI Engineer
highlights: When I joined Bavest, the company provided financial data and analytics products to financial institutions. My primary responsibility was building a new capability: forecasting future company performance rather than only reporting historical data.

I designed and developed Bavest's first machine-learning forecasting platform for fundamental financial metrics such as earnings per share, EBITDA, and EBIT. The project covered the entire forecasting stack: point-in-time feature engineering, model development, historical backtesting, evaluation methodology, experimentation infrastructure, and production deployment. The system was benchmarked against analyst consensus across thousands of historical observations, produces forecasts even for companies that analysts do not cover, and became the foundation for Bavest's forecasting products.

Alongside the forecasting platform, I owned several production data systems that formed part of the company's core infrastructure: a macroeconomic data platform integrating official sources across multiple countries, a financial-metrics engine serving more than 100 analytics metrics through customer-facing APIs, and components of the firm's ETF data platform.
The work spanned machine-learning research, data engineering, and product development in a small team, with end-to-end ownership from experimentation through production operation.

experience.label: Industry Project
highlights: Developed an AI-driven cybersecurity training platform for the State of Hessen with adaptive learning paths and chatbot-based training. (Led by Prof. Dr. Massoth)

experience.label: Intercultural Tutor

entries.name: Titanic - Machine Learning from Disaster
entries.description: A stacking ensemble pipeline for the Kaggle Titanic competition, built around leak-free feature engineering (leave-one-out group survival encoding, data-driven K-means groupings) and Bayesian hyperparameter tuning across six diverse base learners — near the practical ML ceiling for this dataset.
